<summary>Agent "Find next batch of dev users" completed</summary>
<result>@MaverickDots: quant/markets-focused (finance pundit) — explicitly excluded by the rules. Drop.

I now have a verified list of 13 solid candidates. Let me compile the final report. Let me re-pull the exact pain-post quotes I need from the search links I captured. I have all the data from the captures. Let me organize.

Verified candidates (all DM-open confirmed, none built a competing memory tool):

Strong/hands-on devs under ~15k:
1. @seelffff (7.1k) — strongest pain quote
2. @NicholasPuru (903)
3. @jturntdev (449)
4. @ScatterLabz (97)
5. @mlcarldev (33)
6. @deforestpeg (1.4k)
7. @daryl_ngako (13)
8. @LawandOps (25)
9. @ArnoldCartagena (41)
10. @victor_explore (19.8k)
11. @gidim (520)
12. @UncleJAI (7.3k)
13. @cliff_marquez (16) — weak (business-side)
14. @dominikmartn (3.2k) — weak (designer, weaker pain alignment)

That's 14. Let me write the final report sorted by fit + likelihood to adopt.

I have everything verified on-page. Here is the final deliverable.

---

## Kage DM targets — 14 verified developers voicing the exact pain

All handles, follower counts, quotes, and DM-status verified on-page in the logged-in X session (2026-06-18). None have built their own coding-agent memory tool. All known competitors/promoters (claude-mem/$CMEM, mem0, supermemory, Memanto, Oneiro, Headroom, SubQ, Qodo, OpenClacky, AgentArchitect, KijAkubovs) and finance/news/influencer accounts were filtered out.

### ⭐ Best 8 first DMs (top fit + most likely to install)

| # | Name | @handle | ~Followers | Pain post (quote + link) | DM open? | Built own tool? |
|---|------|---------|-----------|--------------------------|----------|-----------------|
| 1 | self.dll | **@seelffff** | 7,101 | "claude ran in full amnesia mode every session from scratch – 10-15 minutes average just to re-explain context on an 80,000 line project, that's ~2 hours a day wasted" — https://x.com/seelffff/status/2044471881114267725 | Yes (bio: "DM is open") | No (AI/ML engineer) |
| 2 | Nick Puru | **@NicholasPuru** | 903 | "I've run Claude Code on client work for 2 years. Past a certain size it forgets your codebase and edits the wrong files. I blamed the model every time. The model was never the problem." — https://x.com/NicholasPuru/status/2062195055306350782 | Yes | No (co-founder Reprise/Sync2 — unrelated) |
| 3 | J J | **@jturntdev** | 449 | "Selective context engineering needs to not be a choice. But to become your doctrine… Jurisdiction is controlled context selection." (heavy Codex/Claude builder, "x100 Engineer") — https://x.com/jturntdev/status/2057577930029453582 | Yes | No (building @tradetir) |
| 4 | djaas_factory | **@ScatterLabz** | 97 | "Include an instruction to keep updating itself (CLAUDE.md) as project evolves." (solo SaaS indie hacker, 5 apps/5 months, "AI aggressively") — https://x.com/ScatterLabz/status/2067223702131921148 | Yes | No |
| 5 | Noonien Soong | **@mlcarldev** | 33 | "This is exactly what you don't want to deal with when you have such a mammoth project. This is the second or third time this has happened since yesterday…" (context loss on a big project) — https://x.com/mlcarldev/status/2067272381790396539 | Yes | No (builds agent personas) |
| 6 | clay | **@deforestpeg** | 1,359 | "I wired in codex to pick the objectives and claude to execute them… that's exactly the problem" (multi-agent builder hitting continuity/cost) — https://x.com/deforestpeg/status/2065896207059152989 | Yes | No (builds SpendLens — not memory) |
| 7 | Daryl Ngako | **@daryl_ngako** | 13 | "Your AI forgets everything you teach it… My fix: a 'second brain' built with Obsidian + Claude Code." (hand-rolled markdown memory — prime upgrade target) — https://x.com/daryl_ngako/status/2067171312506720684 | Yes | No (DIY workflow only) |
| 8 | Lawand Soran | **@LawandOps** | 25 | Vibe-coding journey, "ML enthusiast… AI Agent development. Turning complex ideas into working systems." (tiny, hands-on, exact ICP) — https://x.com/LawandOps/status/2066977553110200741 | Yes | No |

### Next tier (good, slightly weaker fit/quote)

| # | Name | @handle | ~Followers | Pain post (quote + link) | DM open? | Built own tool? |
|---|------|---------|-----------|--------------------------|----------|-----------------|
| 9 | Arnold Cartagena | **@ArnoldCartagena** | 41 | "specs and markdown files, reviewing what my agents produced, writing feedback, pasting it back, going again… a loop I was running by hand, dozens of times a day." — https://x.com/ArnoldCartagena/status/2066887874172145820 | Yes | No, but builds his own "harness/factory" (DIY-minded — mild risk) |
| 10 | Victor | **@victor_explore** | 19,800 | "AGENTS md is solving the configuration mess… instead of separate files for Cursor, Claude Code, Kilo Code, one standardized file." (indie hacker, builds w/ agents) — https://x.com/victor_explore/status/1969057669194432649 | Yes | No |
| 11 | Gideon M | **@gidim** | 520 | "amazing how much the Claude Code/Codex default settings are designed to just burn tokens… dozens of config changes drop token consumption." — https://x.com/gidim/status/2066895445775761745 | Yes | No memory tool (is CEO of Comet/Opik — LLM observability; adjacent vendor, slight risk) |
| 12 | Uncle J | **@UncleJAI** | 7,254 | "Stable project context… remove the tax of re-explaining the work every session." (pivoting to agentic engineering) — https://x.com/UncleJAI/status/2067267368565538880 | Yes | No (more org/process than pure dev) |

### Lower tier (include only if you need volume — weaker ICP)

| # | Name | @handle | ~Followers | Pain post (quote + link) | DM open? | Built own tool? |
|---|------|---------|-----------|--------------------------|----------|-----------------|
| 13 | Cliff Marquez | **@cliff_marquez** | 16 | "Small, actually-doable tips to make your AI workflow smoother… I come from the business side." (operator, not core dev) — https://x.com/cliff_marquez/status/2066987525819047996 | Yes | No |
| 14 | Dom | **@dominikmartn** | 3,182 | "i don't get the hype around cursor. why pay extra to wrap models when i can just hit them directly and not burn tokens" (designer-who-builds; pain is token cost, not memory) — https://x.com/dominikmartn/status/2067230531323060583 | Yes | No |

### Notable EXCLUSIONS (verified, do not DM)
- **Competitors / built-own memory tools:** @Claude_Memory/$CMEM, @VaibhavSisinty (promoting claude-mem), @mem0ai, @supermemory, @so_sthbryan (Memanto), @MBrezauckis (Oneiro), @PeerReview (SuperPaste), @Zev_ee (Headroom), @python_spaces (Qodo), @LDstartupnotes (OpenClacky), @suhailw_dev (AgentArchitect), @KijAkubovs86334, @techinevolution (building memory system).
- **Too big / news / influencer / off-ICP:** @RoundtableSpace (253k, Mario Nawfal), @aibytekat (36k, AI-tools content), @PrajwalTomar_ (55k, MVP agency), @RodmanAi (33.7k, promo account), @Dipanshu_AI (AI-news curation), @charliejhills (AI-content for entrepreneurs, uses Claude for infographics not code).
- **Off-target:** @MaverickDots (quant/markets pundit), @YourPrivateProx (proxy vendor), @BrenBuilds (actively argues against memory plugins — would resist adoption).

### Notes / caveats
- DM-open status reflects a visible Message button or an explicit "DM open" bio at check time; X can still gate DMs by follower relationship, so a few may bounce.
- Follower counts are on-page snapshots and drift over time.
- @daryl_ngako and @ArnoldCartagena are especially clean "hand-rolled markdown/notes" upgrade pitches — they feel the pain and have only a DIY fix. @ArnoldCartagena's self-built "harness" is the one mild risk (DIY-builder mindset).
- @gidim runs an LLM-observability company (Opik) — adjacent, not a direct memory competitor, but pitch as complementary.</result>
